title: Unit 05 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Political Participation, Elections, and Media.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of voting in an election?

choices.text: To choose leaders and influence government decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. Voting allows citizens to select leaders and influence government.
Incorrect. Laws are created by elected officials.
Incorrect. Voting does not replace the Constitution.
Incorrect. Courts are separate from voting decisions.

questions.prompt: Elections are the process used to choose government leaders.

choices.feedback: Correct. Elections are used to select leaders.
Incorrect. That is the purpose of elections.

questions.prompt: What is the main role of political parties?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Enforcement is a role of the executive branch.

choices.text: To elect candidates and organize government leadership
choices.feedback: Correct. Political parties focus on elections and leadership.
Incorrect. Courts interpret laws.
Incorrect. The Constitution was created historically, not by parties.

questions.prompt: What do interest groups mainly try to do?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the role of elections and parties.

choices.text: Influence public policy and government decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. Interest groups advocate for specific issues.
Incorrect. Interest groups and parties serve different roles.
Incorrect. That is not their role.

questions.prompt: Media can influence how people think about political issues.

choices.feedback: Correct. Media shapes how people understand information.
Incorrect. Media does influence public opinion.

choices.text: The decisions made by government officials
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Public opinion refers to people's views.

choices.text: The beliefs and views people hold about issues
choices.feedback: Correct. Public opinion is what people think about issues.
Incorrect. That is legislation.
Incorrect. That is judicial action.

questions.prompt: Voting is the only way citizens can participate in government.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. There are many forms of civic participation.
Correct. Citizens can participate in many ways beyond voting.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of civic participation beyond voting?

choices.text: Serving on a jury or volunteering in the community
choices.feedback: Correct. Civic participation includes actions beyond voting.
Incorrect. That is not participation.
Incorrect. That reduces participation.

choices.text: Only watching political news without action
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Participation requires action.
